title: Packages, Repositories, Dependencies, and Software Provenance · Command Brief: Package Manager Model
summary: Install or update a required tool while preserving repository trust, version evidence, rollback options, and the boundary between system and project dependencies. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux package manager model increment in a distribution-aware package change record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish repositories, metadata, packages, dependencies, installed files, scripts, and transaction history.
Avoid exposing secrets through environment, history, process arguments, logs, or world-readable startup files.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Package Manager Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host proposes lab-tool 2.4.1 from a signed distribution repository and 2.5.0 from an unverified script. Inspect origin, dependencies, installed files, service impact, and rollback before choosing.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Package Manager Model contract for the supplied data.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Capture identity, path, process, file, and service state; predict parsing and scope; run one safe simulated observation; interpret exit status; then change one bounded layer and verify rollback.
